compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Pink-elephant style review (per-file, positive-first phrasing, short direct prohibitions, no elaborated negative examples):
1. examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md — AFFIRMED. Every section leads with the positive instruction (inventory order-of-preference, contract card fields, tier table, contract-level oracle examples, deterministic sync mechanisms); prohibitions are single sentences (e.g. 'Existence, non-emptiness, and no-exception checks are not oracles'); no Bad:/Wrong: blocks (deterministic greps pass).
2. examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md — AFFIRMED. Step 4 rewrite states the design order positively; Step 5 additions name what to hand over and which mutation to seed; pre-existing prohibition style untouched.
3. examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md — AFFIRMED. Phase 2 bullet qualified with the quantified standard, stated affirmatively.
4. examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md — AFFIRMED. 4a/4b additions direct the reader to the standard and state the oracle minimum positively.
5. examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md — AFFIRMED. New paragraph is an affirmative read-this-when trigger plus the planning-readiness rule.
6. examples/claude-skills/README.md — AFFIRMED. Convention documented as instructions (edit canonical, run sync); one-line statement that drift cannot land.
All text is addressed to the LLM user of the skill.

compiled_state.description:
## What

Create `examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md` — the shared TDD test-design standard — and wire it into the three skills at the point each incorporates testing. Written in the skills' voice, with the 'scale ceremony to the bug' principle explicit.

**Prompt style (applies to every prompt change in this task).** All added/changed prompt text addresses the LLM user of the skill, not a human audience, and avoids the pink-elephant problem: lead with positive guidance (what to do, with concrete correct examples), keep prohibitions short and direct, and never elaborate negative examples that prime the model toward the prohibited behavior. Concise throughout; no narration of wrong approaches beyond a one-line prohibition.

**Reference mechanism (canonical file + byte-identical per-skill copies, drift-gated).** `shared/test-design.md` is the canonical copy; each consuming skill directory ships a REAL FILE `<skill>/test-design.md` that must be byte-identical to it. Every SKILL.md references `test-design.md` in its OWN directory — the same same-directory companion-file pattern rebar-debug already uses for `llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md` — so a skill resolves the standard in every load context (repo checkout, a symlinked skill dir in a user skills location, a plain copy, a zip download, any OS), with no symlinks and no user-/machine-specific paths anywhere in the repo. Divergence is prevented by a new gating unit test `tests/unit/test_skill_shared_sync.py` that asserts each copy is byte-identical to the canonical file — it runs under `make test`, an existing gating CI command, mirroring the repo's regenerate-and-diff drift-gate convention (prompt-index, public-types, env-vars). Symlinks were considered and rejected: Windows checkouts without symlink support and GitHub zip archives materialize a symlink as a one-line text file, and copying a single skill directory leaves it dangling. The README documents the convention: edit the canonical file, then sync the copies (`for s in rebar-debug rebar-implement rebar-brainstorm; do cp examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md examples/claude-skills/$s/test-design.md; done`). It names the consuming skills; rebar-janitor is deliberately NOT a consumer — it never authors or runs tests (it finds, verifies, and files maintainability work as tickets; its phase docs own its ticketization criteria), so it takes no copy, and the drift test hardcodes the same three-skill consumer set (adding a consumer means adding it to the test and the one-liner together).

The shared file covers:
- Existing-coverage inventory before authoring: the required artifact is a RED oracle, which may be an existing failing test, an existing test strengthened into RED form, or a new test only when nothing covers the behavior. Search by contract wording, public entry point, implementation symbol, error text, and bug/regression identifier; read the nearest tests.
- A test contract card frozen before test code, with scaled fields: contract + citation, bug-triggering preconditions, production path, tier + why lower is insufficient, observable postcondition, CI gate are always required; negative control, collateral invariants, recovery, fault injection apply conditionally (stateful/atomicity/concurrency work).
- Test-tier selection rule: the smallest tier that still crosses every component in the proven mechanism, with the bug-class -> tier table pairing each class with its tier: pure calculation or validation branch -> in-process unit test; producer/consumer shape mismatch -> contract test through real serialization; CLI parsing/streams/env/exit codes -> real CLI subprocess; git refs/merge/reachability/data loss -> real temporary git repos; locking/race -> multiple real processes with a deterministic barrier; parent exit/signals/fd inheritance -> parent-child subprocess; crash recovery -> kill-and-restart at a failpoint; external-service mapping -> stateful verified fake; LLM instruction behavior -> behavioral eval. Work spanning several bug classes takes the tier per mechanism-component. At planning time the tier rule is a readiness check, never a deferral: acceptance criteria name both the observable oracle and the tier, and a plan whose mechanism is too unclear to pick a tier carries an unresolved design decision that must be resolved (research, a spike, a clarifying question) before the ticket is recorded.
- 'Trigger the mechanism, assert the contract': arrange conditions that force the mechanism through the smallest production path crossing the faulty seam; assert the public/contractual consequence, never mock call counts, private helpers, intermediate payloads, or source text.
- Minimum behavioral oracle: prove the fixture preconditions; assert an exact behavioral postcondition (not is-not-None / nonempty / no-exception); collateral invariants for stateful operations; a clean retry where the contract is atomicity/recoverability.
- Contrast case: default for input-class defects and stateful operations (nearest passing control / second representative input, parameterized where a family applies); justified omission allowed for trivially single-point bugs.
- Fault realism: the injected fault must occur inside the code whose recovery/cleanup is under test (fail the real second rename, not a top-level wrapper); race tests must synchronize with a barrier/pipe/marker/failpoint, never timing-only sleeps as the primary mechanism.
- Prove the test gates: the runner collects the node; it runs (not SKIP/XFAIL) in the supported CI environment; any temporary xfail is strict=True with a removal condition; the test runs under a gating CI command.
- Defect-seeded mutation: derive the mutation from the confirmed root cause (re-introduce the exact defect), not an arbitrary negation.
- LLM-surface evals: pinned model/settings, paired before/after prompts, multiple samples with a predeclared threshold and reported failure rates, plus a negative control — quantified rates, never one RED and one GREEN sample. One narrow carve-out: a single-sample RED/GREEN pair is acceptable ONLY when the assertion is deterministic under the pinned settings (e.g. schema/rule-based scoring at temperature 0) and the report states that determinism; cost or inconvenience is never grounds for skipping quantification.

Skill integration (each SKILL.md references `test-design.md` in its own directory, resolved by its committed byte-identical copy):
- `examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md`: Step 4 becomes 'Design and prove a RED oracle' — inventory first, contract card, tier rule, then RED with proven preconditions, loading the shared file at Phase 2 entry. Step 4's existing LLM-surface paragraph is updated to cite the shared file's quantified-eval requirements as the governing RED-artifact standard, superseding its current single-eval description. Step 5's mutation check becomes defect-seeded, and the fixer handoff list gains a fourth item — the frozen contract card (intended postcondition + invariants) — appended after 'confirmed root cause, evidence, original reproduction steps', while test fixture/assertions stay held out. `llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md`'s Phase 2 (Repair) bullet — whose current wording defines RED as a single failing eval/behavioural assertion — is updated to cite `test-design.md`'s quantified-eval requirements (multiple samples, predeclared threshold, negative control) as the governing RED-artifact standard, so the two files cannot disagree.
- `examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md`: Phase 4a gains an explicit instruction sentence — before deriving the specification, work from `test-design.md` in this skill's directory for oracle design (coverage inventory, tier selection, oracle completeness, gating proof) — and Phase 4b's authoring text cites it for the oracle-minimum checks; the held-out structure (4c/4d/4e) is unchanged.
- `examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md`: in the existing 'Before recording tickets, read the project's plan-review gate' section, add: before recording any ticket whose acceptance criteria will include or reference testing, verification, or behavioral oracles, read the shared file — when in doubt, read it (the cost is one file read; the benefit is correctly-shaped ACs that name the observable oracle).
- `examples/claude-skills/README.md`: document the shared/ + copies convention (canonical file, byte-identical copies, the sync one-liner, the gating drift test).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md` exists as a regular file and covers every bullet above (inventory, contract card, tier table + per-mechanism-component rule + planning-readiness (no tier deferral) rule, trigger/assert rule, oracle minimum, contrast default, fault realism, gating proof, defect-seeded mutation, LLM evals) with the scale-to-the-bug principle stated. Prove: `for t in 'RED oracle' 'contract card' 'tier' 'collateral' 'fault' 'gating' 'defect-seeded'; do grep -qi "$t" examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md || { echo "MISSING $t"; exit 1; }; done` plus a section-by-section read against the bullet list.
- [ ] For each of rebar-debug, rebar-implement, rebar-brainstorm: `examples/claude-skills/<skill>/test-design.md` is a regular file byte-identical to the canonical copy. Prove: `for s in rebar-debug rebar-implement rebar-brainstorm; do cmp examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md examples/claude-skills/$s/test-design.md; done` succeeds and `git ls-files -s examples/claude-skills | grep 120000` is empty.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_skill_shared_sync.py` asserts byte-identity of every copy against the canonical file AND self-verifies its detection logic: a second test case builds a perturbed copy in `tmp_path` and asserts the same comparison detects the divergence (so the RED path is machine-verified on every CI run, not human-attested). Prove: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_skill_shared_sync.py -q` green, with both the identity case and the detection case collected.
- [ ] rebar-debug Step 4 is retitled to RED-oracle design. Prove: `grep -n '^## Step 4.*RED oracle' examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md` returns a match.
- [ ] rebar-debug Step 4's LLM-surface paragraph defers to the shared eval standard. Prove: `awk '/^## Step 4/,/^## Step 5/' examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md | grep -q 'test-design.md' && awk '/^## Step 4/,/^## Step 5/' examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md | grep -q 'LLM-surface'` (section-scoped, window-free).
- [ ] rebar-debug Step 5 hands the fixer the contract card as a fourth handoff item without exposing held-out test code. Prove: `awk '/^## Step 5/,/^## Step 6/' examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md | grep -q 'contract card'` (section-scoped), and reading the handoff sentence confirms it enumerates root cause, evidence, reproduction steps, contract card — no test path/file.
- [ ] `llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md`'s Phase 2 (Repair) bullet is QUALIFIED (not deleted or fully rephrased): it keeps its RED-artifact definition and adds a citation that quantification follows `test-design.md`. Prove: `awk '/Phase 2 \(Repair\)/,/^$/' examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md | grep -q 'test-design.md'` (bullet-scoped).
- [ ] rebar-debug Step 5's mutation check is defect-seeded. Prove: `grep -n 'defect-seeded\|re-introduce' examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md` hits inside the mutation-check bullet.
- [ ] rebar-implement Phase 4a/4b cite the shared file inside those sections (content-level placement, not a footnote). Prove: `awk '/^### 4a/,/^### 4c/' examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md | grep -q 'test-design.md'` succeeds and the cited sentence names the oracle-design duties (inventory, tier, oracle completeness, gating proof).
- [ ] rebar-brainstorm's 'Before recording tickets' section directs reading the shared file with the when-in-doubt trigger. Prove: `grep -n -C3 'test-design.md' examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md` shows the hit inside that section.
- [ ] `examples/claude-skills/README.md` documents the convention — BOTH facts proven separately: `grep -q 'byte-identical' examples/claude-skills/README.md && grep -q 'cp examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md' examples/claude-skills/README.md`.
- [ ] No user-/machine-specific paths. Prove: `git grep -n '/Users/\|joeoakhart' -- examples/claude-skills tests/unit/test_skill_shared_sync.py` returns nothing.
- [ ] Style check, two parts. Deterministic: `! grep -nE '^(Bad|Wrong):' examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md` and `! grep -n 'do NOT assert' examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md` (no negative-example blocks or shouted negative enumerations in the shared file). Artifact: a comment on this ticket lists all six touched prompt files with a per-file affirmation of positive-first phrasing and short direct prohibitions; prove the comment exists via `rebar show 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900`.
- [ ] `make lint && make test` pass (the drift test gates via `make test`, CI's single source of truth; the targeted node `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_skill_shared_sync.py -q` is additionally run to show both its cases collected).

file_impact.reason: Phase 2 RED-artifact cites quantified eval standard

compiled_state.title: Shared TDD test-design reference (examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md) + integrate into the three skills

verify_commands.command: test -f examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md && for s in rebar-debug rebar-implement rebar-brainstorm; do cmp -s examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md examples/claude-skills/$s/test-design.md || exit 1; done && grep -l "test-design.md" examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md
verify_commands.dd_text: canonical file exists; each skill copy is byte-identical; SKILL.mds reference it
drift test collected and passing; RED when a copy diverges
